result:
If I could change the world in one way, I would make education free, equal, and accessible to all. I believe that education is the great equalizer and has the power to lift people out of poverty, reduce inequality, foster critical thinking and empathy, and help solve many of the world's greatest challenges. 

In my ideal world, every child would have access to high quality education from pre-school through college, regardless of their socioeconomic status, race, gender, or geographic location. Schools would be well-funded and staffed with passionate, highly-trained teachers. Curricula would emphasize not just basic skills, but also creativity, collaboration, civic engagement, and social-emotional development.

Higher education and vocational training would be affordable for all, with a wide range of programs to prepare people for success in diverse careers. Lifelong learning would be supported and encouraged.

I believe unleashing the full potential of human minds through universal education could lead to a more prosperous, peaceful, innovative and equitable world. Ensuring every person has the opportunity to learn, grow and contribute their talents is in my view one of the most powerful levers we have to shape a better future.

Of course, making this vision a reality is immensely challenging. But if I could wave a magic wand, establishing free, high-quality education as a fundamental human right would be my top priority for changing the world. Education has ripple effects that can transform individuals, communities and entire societies for the better.
